title: Argparse Interface, Help, Options, and Usage Errors · Behavior Case: Argument Model
summary: The parser abbreviates ambiguous flags, hides defaults and units, accepts negative limits, prints colored help into snapshots, and couples parse_args directly to analysis. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the argument model increment in testable user-facing command interface. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.
Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-argparse-interface-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-argparse-interface-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_argparse_interface_5(arguments: int = 6, invalid_arguments: int = 3, invalid_rejected: int = 3, help_has_units: bool = True, abbreviations_disabled: bool = True, color_controlled: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if arguments < 1 or invalid_arguments < 0:
        return False, arguments, "argument-shape"
    if invalid_rejected != invalid_arguments:
        return False, invalid_rejected, "argument-validation"
    if not help_has_units:
        return False, arguments, "help-contract"
    if not abbreviations_disabled or not color_controlled:
        return False, arguments, "parser-reproducibility"
    assert invalid_rejected == invalid_arguments
    return True, arguments, "testable-cli"

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Argument Model model · Theory Argument Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Every implementation setting belongs in the public CLI.
